paper_title: OC EO CULTURE: A CASE STUDY OF OC EO ARTIFACTS IN SOUTHERN VIETNAM

entities.description: Author of the research paper from Can Tho University.
Educational institution located in Vietnam where the author is affiliated.
The primary field of study concerning Oc Eo artifacts and culture.
An early culture formed from the first to the sixth centuries in Southern Vietnam.
Simple, hand-made objects representing the Oc Eo culture.
The East-West trade route where Oc Eo served as an important transit port.
Method of uncovering artifacts and architectural remains from the earth.
Scientific method used to determine the age of organic materials like wooden statues.
Classification and study of ceramic remains to understand indigenous traditions.
Use of written documents and records to discover traits of the culture.
The specific age range determined for wooden Buddha statues.
The measured average height of ancient Oc Eo men based on skull findings.
The measured average height of ancient Oc Eo women based on skull findings.
The physical size of the rectangular temple structure found at Nen Chua.
The measurement range for the length of Oc Eo tombs.
The specific number of gold objects listed in Malleret's inventory.

relations.source: OC EO CULTURE: A CASE STUDY OF OC EO ARTIFACTS IN SOUTHERN VIETNAM
